element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Running, jumping, helmet bash through walls and enemies
Sword combat, bow and arrows for distance/switch activation
Shield blocking, shield hang-gliding, shield platforming for allies
Learning consequences of enemy contact and fall damage
Using one Viking's ability to enable another's progression
All three characters must reach exit; failure of one blocks level completion
Timing and positioning with limited health

typical_relief_triggers: Safe room with only one character needed
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating between solo character platforming challenges and multi-character puzzle sequences; tension builds through enemy density and coordination complexity; relief through safe exploration periods

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of individual character abilities in isolation; safe exploration with minimal enemy encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing complexity of multi-character coordination puzzles; moderate enemy density; health management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced synchronization requirements; higher enemy density; complex environmental hazards combined with combat demands
notable_spikes: Transition from solo character tutorials to mandatory two-character cooperation
Levels requiring all three characters in simultaneous action sequences
Silly level set (Level 26 onward) with thematic difficulty escalation

notable_level_patterns.description: One Viking must traverse a section solo while others are unable to help; tests individual mastery of character's unique mechanic
Olaf's shield must be used as a standing platform for Erik to reach heights or for Baleog to cross gaps; establishes dependency chains
Baleog's bow must hit distant switches while other Vikings navigate resulting puzzle state changes
Multiple enemies with limited resources (health, items) requiring tactical character rotation and health food consumption
Confrontation with Tomator requiring all three Vikings' abilities in coordinated pattern; combines all mechanics learned across 37 levels

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Lost Vikings SNES page)
